sources.notes: Statement and constraints: maintain a colored weighted tree under vertex recolor queries; after each query output total weight of edges whose endpoints have different colors.
Official editorial/code roots the tree and stores, for each vertex, sums of child-edge weights grouped by child color to update answers.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate checked by search/open attempt; not used as a consulted solution source.

annotation.summary: A tree has colored vertices and weighted edges. After each recolor query, output the sum of weights of edges whose endpoints have different colors.
annotation.constraints: Sum n and q are large; total per-query work must avoid scanning the whole tree.
annotation.core_idea: Root the tree. Maintain the global bad-edge weight. For every vertex v, store a map from color to total weight of child edges leading to that color. Recoloring v changes all child edges by cnt[v][old]-cnt[v][new], and changes the parent edge separately. Also update v's contribution in its parent's color map.
annotation.complexity: O((n+q) log n) with ordered maps, or expected O(n+q) with hash maps
tricks: rooted tree turns one incident edge into parent plus child buckets

solution_variants.name: rooted-child-color-maps
solution_variants.summary: DFS to root the tree, initialize child color sums and global answer, then update only v's child bucket sums and parent edge on each recolor.

solution_variants.name: heavy-degree-color-cache
solution_variants.summary: Alternative common implementation stores color sums only for high-degree vertices and scans low-degree adjacency lists.

tags.evidence: The update formula relies on rooting the tree and separating parent and child edges.
Each vertex needs a color-keyed map of adjacent child edge weights to update recolors efficiently.
A DFS roots the tree and initializes parent edges, child edge weights, and color buckets.
A known alternative groups vertices by degree and caches only heavy-neighbor color sums.
